author: Joshua Franklin
title: Jamie Dimon warns private credit losses will be larger than feared - Financial Times
description: JPMorgan chief raises alarm on weakening lending standards in annual shareholder letter

author: Patrick Coffee
title: Brands Adopt ‘No AI’ Disclaimers to Stand Out Amid the Slop - WSJ

author: Jen Glantz
title: I spend $12,000 a year on Botox, hair, nails, and fitness. In Utah, they're necessary — especially in PR. - Business Insider
description: Hilary Reiter Azzaretti had never done Botox or gotten regular facials before moving to Utah. Once she saw the people in Park City, she started both.

author: Pranav Dixit, Ashley Rodriguez
title: Meta salary data reveals a VP of AI can make $650,000 in base salary - Business Insider
description: See how much Meta paid in 2025, with software engineers earning as much as $450,000. Salary data shows pay ranges across over 5,000 roles.

author: Malavika Kaur Makol, Srinivasan Sivabalan
title: Emerging Market Stocks, Currencies Gain on Iran Ceasefire Report - Bloomberg.com
description: Emerging-market stocks and currencies rose for a second day as investors weighed a report that mediators are making efforts to secure a ceasefire in the Iran war.

author: Shubhangi Goel
title: Anthropic's head of growth says the company culture is so open that people 'just argue with Dario' on Slack - Business Insider
description: Anthropic's head of growth, Amol Avasare, said the AI lab has such an open culture that employees can give feedback to top leadership.

author: Anne D'Innocenzio
title: Tailors and dressmakers retire their pincushions as US demand for skilled sewers grows - AP News
description: The ranks of tailors, dressmakers and custom sewers are shrinking in the U.S. even as their skills and services are finding fresh demand. Fashion industry experts say younger shoppers are enlisting tailors and seamstresses to give off-the-rack purchases a cus…
